description: Curated cancer predisposition gene panel for Yeliztli (P3-12). Covers 22 gene groups (28 individual genes) associated with hereditary cancer syndromes, with expected ClinVar Pathogenic/Likely Pathogenic entries.

genes.name: BRCA1 DNA Repair Associated
syndromes: Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome
genes.notes: BRCA1 variants produce findings in both cancer and carrier modules. Dual-role gene with distinct framing per module.

genes.name: BRCA2 DNA Repair Associated
syndromes: Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome
genes.notes: BRCA2 variants produce findings in both cancer and carrier modules. Dual-role gene with distinct framing per module.

genes.name: Tumor Protein P53
genes.notes: Li-Fraumeni syndrome with multi-organ cancer risk. Early onset cancer common.

genes.name: Partner and Localizer of BRCA2
genes.notes: Moderate-to-high penetrance hereditary breast cancer gene (partner and localizer of BRCA2; homologous-recombination repair). Associated cancer types (#442): breast, ovarian, and pancreatic. Breast is established and high-magnitude - population-scale case-control consortia not selected on family history (CARRIERS/BRIDGES, BC OR ~3.9-4.7, PMID 33471991) agree with family-based segregation (Yang 2020 RR 7.18, PMID 31841383), and CARRIERS-informed iCARE puts cumulative breast-cancer risk to age 80 in the BRCA2 range, strongly modified by family history (O'Brien 2025 JAMA Oncol, PMID 41066089). Male breast cancer is also an established PALB2 association (Yang 2020 RR 7.34, PMID 31841383; ACMG 2021, PMID 33976419) - represented under the breast type. Pancreatic is general (Yang 2020 RR 2.37, PMID 31841383) and ACMG 2021 endorses considering pancreatic surveillance (PMID 33976419). Ovarian is retained as a GENERAL but MODERATE, lower-magnitude association (Yang 2020 RR 2.91, PMID 31841383; Narayan 2023 meta-analysis OR 2.48, PMID 37651980; Song 2019 high-grade-serous OR 3.01, PMID 32546565; three unselected population cohorts OR 5.79, Kotsopoulos 2023, PMID 37493628) - but the magnitude is modest, ACMG 2021 does NOT recommend routine ovarian surveillance and only rarely considers risk-reducing salpingo-oophorectomy before age 50 (PMID 33976419), and the earliest ovarian case-control was non-significant (Ramus 2015 P=.08). IMPORTANT conditional caveat: prostate cancer is intentionally NOT a general associated type. PALB2 confers NO increased risk of OVERALL prostate cancer in the family-based international study (Yang 2020, no evidence for prostate or colorectal risk, PMID 31841383) and is non-significant for overall prostate cancer in large biobank/consortium meta-analyses; the prostate signal is restricted to AGGRESSIVE/high-grade/lethal disease - Wokolorczyk 2021 found overall prostate OR 1.38 (NS) but high-Gleason OR 8.05 and worse survival (PMID 34006922), mirrored by Darst 2020 (aggressive OR 6.31). Prostate is therefore a variant/phenotype-conditional association documented here only, not added to cancer_types. Colorectal is likewise NOT associated (Yang 2020 found no increased colorectal risk, PMID 31841383) and is correctly absent. PMID audit note: the prior pmids list carried 24136374, which resolves to an unrelated plant nickel-tolerance paper (NOT PALB2) and must be replaced; 33471991 is correctly the CARRIERS/BRIDGES breast-cancer-risk-genes analysis.

genes.name: ATM Serine/Threonine Kinase
genes.notes: Moderate (intermediate) penetrance gene; cancer risk behaves as a continuous variable modified by family history and other factors (ACMG resource, Pal 2024 Genet Med, PMID 39636577). General associated cancer types (#442): breast, pancreatic, and prostate — the ACMG-established ATM triad. Breast is established at the population scale (ATM protein-truncating variant OR ~2, lifetime ~20-30% by age 80) in cohorts NOT selected on family history (BCAC >113,000 women, Dorling 2021 NEJM, PMID 33471991; CARRIERS population study, Hu 2021 NEJM, PMID 33471974). Pancreatic is confirmed by a multicenter penetrance study (RR 6.5, ~9.5% cumulative risk by age 80; Hsu 2021 JAMA Oncol, PMID 34529012) and is the highest-relative-risk cancer for ATM PTV carriers in UK Biobank whole-exome data (Mukhtar 2024 J Med Genet, PMID 39209703). Prostate is confirmed by the PRACTICAL Consortium (tier-1 variant OR 4.4 with earlier onset; Karlsson 2021 Eur Urol Oncol, PMID 33436325), by 7636 unselected Japanese prostate cases vs 12,366 controls (OR 2.86; Momozawa 2020 JNCI, PMID 31214711), and in UK Biobank (PMID 39209703). IMPORTANT high-risk-variant caveat: the kinase-inactivating missense allele c.7271T>G is NOT attenuated but instead carries roughly double the risk of ATM truncating variants — invasive breast RR 4.1-4.6 and prostate RR 4.84 in BCAC + UK Biobank — and warrants variant-specific management (Mukhtar 2025 J Med Genet, PMID 40451289). Conditional (NOT general) types kept out of cancer_types: ovarian is significant only in clinically-ascertained or PTV-specific analyses (Lu 2019 JAMA Oncol OR 2.85, PMID 30128536; Lilyquist 2017 ascertained ovarian RR >2, PMID 28888541; UK Biobank PTV p<0.001, PMID 39209703) but is NOT an ACMG-established ATM association and risk-reducing salpingo-oophorectomy is not indicated; gastric is elevated mainly for truncating/high-impact variants (Hall 2021 truncating OR 2.97, PMID 33509806; UK Biobank rare-missense p<0.05). Surveillance is itself family-history-modulated per ACMG: enhanced breast surveillance is generally offered (risk-reducing mastectomy generally not recommended), prostate surveillance should be considered, and pancreatic surveillance should be considered based on assessment of family history, ideally within a clinical trial (Pal 2024, PMID 39636577). Older breast meta-analysis retained for context (Marabelli 2016, PMID 27112364).

genes.name: Checkpoint Kinase 2
genes.notes: Moderate-penetrance breast cancer gene; 1100delC is the most-studied variant. Associated cancer types (#393): breast (established; Schmidt 2016 JCO 1100delC risk estimates, PMID 27269948), prostate, thyroid, and kidney. Prostate and kidney are confirmed by population-scale genomic-ascertainment cohorts not selected on family history (Kim 2025 JAMA Netw Open, UK Biobank + Geisinger MyCode: prostate OR 1.62-1.78, kidney OR 1.58-1.84, PMID 41396600; Mukhtar 2024 J Med Genet, UKB WES: prostate and kidney among CHEK2 PTV associations, PMID 39209703). Thyroid (OR 1.63) and kidney (OR 2.57) are also significant in Bychkovsky 2022 (JAMA Oncol, 3,783 carriers — 92% female, so the thyroid estimate is female-weighted; PMID 36136322); the CHEK2 thyroid association is independently corroborated in unselected papillary-thyroid cohorts (Siolek 2015 OR 3.3, PMID 25583358), and CHEK2 is a long-recognised multi-organ susceptibility gene (Cybulski 2004, PMID 15492928). IMPORTANT attenuated-variant caveat: the common missense alleles p.I157T, p.S428F and p.T476M have an attenuated breast association and were NOT associated with any non-breast cancer in Bychkovsky 2022 — the prostate/thyroid/kidney associations apply to other (truncating and non-attenuated missense) CHEK2 pathogenic variants. Colorectal cancer is intentionally NOT a general associated type: recent large cohorts find no overall CRC risk in unselected CHEK2 carriers, which are in fact LESS likely to have colorectal cancer (Bychkovsky 2022 OR 0.62; Mundt 2023 Cancer Genet, PMID 37839337, 'little to no risk'). Any colorectal association is variant-specific (truncating 1100delC, missense I157T) and concentrated in familial/HBCC contexts (Xiang 2011 familial OR 2.80 but sporadic NS, PMID 21807500; Wasielewski 2008 HNPCC OR 4.3; Schreurs 2024 1100delC-family SIR 1.43, PMID 38828701). ACMG recommends colorectal/prostate surveillance be considered based on family history, not unconditionally (Hanson 2023, PMID 37490054).

genes.name: RAD51 Paralog C
genes.notes: RAD51C is a moderate (intermediate) penetrance gene; both panel associations are retained. Ovarian (tubo-ovarian) is the primary, unconditional association: population OR ~5.2-5.6 and segregation RR 7.55 with ~11% lifetime risk to age 80 (PMID 26261251; 32359370; 32107557), confirmed in UK Biobank (PMID 40875208) and by ACMG/UK guidance (PMID 41070818; 36411032). Breast is a genuine general association but ATTENUATED and SUBTYPE-SKEWED: the population-scale effect is modest (overall OR ~1.5-2.0; meta-analysed OR 1.53, 95% CI 1.29-2.04, PMID 38986768; segregation RR 1.99, PMID 32107557) and concentrated in ER-negative/triple-negative disease, where the effect is much stronger (TNBC OR ~6.2, PMID 35084436; CARRIERS ER-negative/TNBC predominance, PMID 33471974). ACMG 2025 frames RAD51C breast risk as moderate and predominantly triple-negative, generally not warranting risk-reducing mastectomy (PMID 41070818). Both ovarian and breast risk are further modulated upward by family history (e.g. up to ~32-36% ovarian / ~44-46% breast with two affected first-degree relatives, PMID 32107557). Prostate and pancreatic are NOT supported as general associations and are deliberately excluded from cancer_types: the prostate signal is limited to a single recurrent missense (A126T) in a regional familial study and is not reproduced in large unbiased cohorts (PMID 40875208), and pancreatic rests on isolated case reports with no population-scale risk estimate.

genes.name: RAD51 Paralog D
genes.notes: RAD51D is a moderate (intermediate) penetrance gene. Ovarian (tubo-ovarian) cancer is the core, unconditional association: RR~7.6 / pooled OR~6.9, ~13% cumulative risk to age 80 (Yang 2020 PMID 32107557; Suszynska 2020 PMID 32359370; confirmed in unselected UK Biobank PMID 40875208; ACMG 2025 PMID 41070818). Breast cancer is a genuine but ATTENUATED population association (overall RR~1.8, meta-analysed population OR 1.76, ~1 in 1079 carriers - among the weakest established BC genes; Rowlands 2024 PMID 38986768) that is PREDOMINANTLY triple-negative/ER-negative (BCAC PMID 33471991; CARRIERS PMID 33471974; BRIDGES TNBC OR~6.2 PMID 35084436; Shimelis 2018 PMID 30099541; ACMG 2025 PMID 41070818). Mirroring the CHEK2 attenuated/low-risk-variant caveat: the breast signal is driven by protein-truncating variants and TNBC, and common recurrent variants (e.g. p.Lys91fs / c.270_271dup) show incomplete penetrance, so breast management is personalized and family-history-aware (UK consensus PMID 36411032; ACMG 2025 PMID 41070818) - generally enhanced surveillance, not risk-reducing mastectomy. Ovarian risk is itself modified by family history (up to ~36% with two affected FDRs; PMID 32107557). Pancreatic and prostate cancer are NOT supported as general RAD51D associations: they rest on small unselected case-series / metastatic cohorts and HRR-gene-set signals (e.g. Pritchard 2016 PMID 27433846) with no population-scale risk estimate, and RAD51D was not individually associated with either in UK Biobank (PMID 40875208) or in ACMG/NCCN RAD51D management - they are documented here only, not added to cancer_types. Citation note: the legacy panel PMID 29446198 (BRCA1/2-family mutational spectrum) does not provide RAD51D risk estimates and should be dropped in favor of the risk-estimate/guideline sources above; 22006311 supports only the ovarian discovery signal.

genes.name: MutL Homolog 1
genes.notes: Lynch syndrome mismatch repair gene. Colonoscopy surveillance recommended for carriers.

genes.name: MutS Homolog 2
genes.notes: Lynch syndrome mismatch repair gene.

genes.name: MutS Homolog 6
genes.notes: Lynch syndrome mismatch repair gene. Somewhat lower penetrance than MLH1/MSH2.

genes.name: PMS1 Homolog 2
genes.notes: Lynch syndrome mismatch repair gene. Lower penetrance than MLH1/MSH2. Pseudogene PMS2CL complicates testing.

genes.name: APC Regulator of WNT Signaling Pathway
genes.notes: Classic Familial Adenomatous Polyposis. Colectomy typically recommended.

genes.name: MutY DNA Glycosylase
genes.notes: Autosomal recessive polyposis. Biallelic carriers have high colorectal cancer risk. Monoallelic carriers have modestly elevated risk.

genes.name: Von Hippel-Lindau Tumor Suppressor
genes.notes: Surveillance recommended for carriers. Renal cell carcinoma screening starting age 15.

genes.name: Ret Proto-Oncogene
genes.notes: Gain-of-function mutations. Prophylactic thyroidectomy recommended for certain variants.

genes.name: Phosphatase and Tensin Homolog
genes.notes: Multi-organ cancer risk. Lifetime breast cancer risk ~85% for female carriers.

genes.name: Serine/Threonine Kinase 11
genes.notes: Peutz-Jeghers syndrome with hamartomatous polyps and elevated multi-organ cancer risk.

genes.name: Cadherin 1
genes.notes: Prophylactic gastrectomy may be recommended. Lobular breast cancer screening for female carriers.

genes.name: Neurofibromin 1
genes.notes: Neurofibromatosis type 1. Cancer risk is part of broader clinical syndrome.

genes.name: Neurofibromin 2 (Merlin)
genes.notes: Bilateral vestibular schwannomas are hallmark. Surveillance MRI recommended.

genes.name: Menin 1
genes.notes: MEN1 classic triad: parathyroid adenoma, pituitary adenoma, pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor.

genes.name: Succinate Dehydrogenase Complex Flavoprotein Subunit A
genes.notes: Lower penetrance compared to SDHB. Surveillance recommended for carriers.

genes.name: Succinate Dehydrogenase Complex Iron Sulfur Subunit B
genes.notes: Higher penetrance and malignancy risk compared to other SDH subunits.

genes.name: Succinate Dehydrogenase Complex Subunit C
genes.notes: Lower penetrance. Head and neck paragangliomas most common.

genes.name: Succinate Dehydrogenase Complex Subunit D
genes.notes: Parent-of-origin effect: disease penetrance primarily with paternal inheritance.

genes.name: BRCA1 Associated Protein 1
genes.notes: BAP1 tumor predisposition syndrome. Dermal melanocytic tumors are common early sign.

genes.name: Cyclin Dependent Kinase Inhibitor 2A
syndromes: Familial Atypical Multiple Mole Melanoma Syndrome (FAMMM)
genes.notes: High penetrance for melanoma in carriers. Pancreatic cancer risk significantly elevated. Evidence (#463): CDKN2A Cancer Predisposition GeneReviews (PMID 40674536); penetrance from Sargen 2022 (JNCI Cancer Spectr, 385 germline-PV carriers across US + Swedish cohorts: melanoma SIR 159.8, pancreatic SIR 24.1; PMID 36269225).
